title: Speculation on Jet Alone
wikitext:
[]: Just what is JA armed with?             Quote Post by EVA-01X » Tue Jun 22, 2004 7:22 pm I always wondered, you know. It dosn't seem like it could hold any weapons in those little hands it's got, but what good against an angel are those? (I know JA' has a ginormous mace and in the fic NXE it had an autocannon)

[]:             Quote Post by wildarmsheero » Tue Jun 22, 2004 7:39 pm It bitch slaps.

[]:             Quote Post by EVA-01X » Tue Jun 22, 2004 7:41 pm Yes :roll: but besides the obvious

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jun 22, 2004 7:42 pm Its armed with a nuclear reactor that can critically explode killing thousands, perhaps millions depending on population density. How's that for armaments?

[]:             Quote Post by x1shinji » Wed Jun 23, 2004 1:45 am werd ^^ :)

[]:             Quote Post by Super Mega Ultra Deluxe » Wed Jun 23, 2004 5:03 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Its armed with a nuclear reactor that can critically explode killing thousands, perhaps millions depending on population density. How's that for armaments?

[]:             Quote Post by tobias » Tue Jun 29, 2004 3:36 pm i think it made a comeback in one of the video games. i'm pretty sure they armed it then...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jun 29, 2004 3:46 pm In the Evangelion 2 game they explained that Jet Alone (as seen in Episode 7) was in fact the second Jet Alone, the first being an impractical prototype model. You can see a schematic for the Jet Alone prototype model from Evangelion 2 below: http://www.evamonkey.com/eva2/technical_drawing_03.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by TheMessiah » Tue Jun 29, 2004 4:40 pm Super Mega Ultra Deluxe wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: Its armed with a nuclear reactor that can critically explode killing thousands, perhaps millions depending on population density. How's that for armaments?

[]:             Quote Post by EVA-01X » Tue Jun 29, 2004 9:21 pm DAmn this really answers nothing....... Okay then what do you people think it would be armed with if you were the desinger of that walking nuke? (Serious stuff please, this is for a fanfic I'm writing)

[]:             Quote Post by Xenogil » Tue Jun 29, 2004 9:38 pm For those of you who don't know Jet Alone's armaments as shown in The Eva Monkey's link, it has a hammer (as you can plainly see) but I believe that the wristband also discharges some type of electric attack. There's an issue of Newtype that I read about it, but I can't remember the exact issue at the moment.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Tue Jul 06, 2004 3:21 pm since the series was terminated, i doubt they developed weapons... its just the eva series...in its first battle eva01 had no weapons...but those claws look pretty nasty...

[]:             Quote Post by EVA-01X » Tue Jul 06, 2004 8:39 pm Yeah but if they continued development I think that they would probably use pretty much the same weapons as the EVAs; Rifles, Bozooka, machineguns and the like....

[]: Jet Alone... could it have worked?             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Tue Oct 21, 2014 12:40 am I know NGE makes Jet Alone look pathetic, but if the first unit hadn't been sabotaged during its demo, and more importantly if its creators had found a way to counter AT fields like they said they were trying to, could it have become an effective weapon against the angels? At first the concept of fighting Angels with robots sounds preposterous, but the very fact that Gendo bothered to sabotage the project seems to indicate that he saw it as a credible threat to his schemes. I'm not going to say that a single Jet Alone would be a match for even the weakest Angel, but perhaps engaging the threat with multipe anti-AT Jet Alones could have been an effective strategy. After all, Jet Alone is much cheaper and easier to produce than an eva unit. They also wouldn't have to worry about finding special children to pilot them. Taking this into account, it would be theoretically possible to produce vast numbers of Jet Alones in a very short time frame, assuming the creators got the funding they needed. Of course, its effectiveness hinges on what types of weapons Jet Alone would have been capable of using, as well as whether or not its biggest issue (the whole "walking bomb" thing) was fixed. But I believe that with the right tweaks, it could have become a decent expendable mass-attack weapon system against some of the more conventional angels. Any thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Oct 21, 2014 12:52 am As an idea, I'll admit that it's a good one. It's kind of like a bulkier version of the dummy system.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionGodMode » Tue Oct 21, 2014 12:56 am Nope, what would it have done against bardiel or armisael who infect the evas. Also gendo and seele sabotaged the jet alone project so the united nations or whoever it was wouldn't get rid of their machines that they wanted to use to rule the world and become god. The jet alone coulda been a good dummy against arael, but to put it up against angels like ramiel and zeruel, plus no A/T field, theres no chance. Also i seem to remember that a couple of the angels where only able to be beaten by Unit 01 going berserk, jet alone can't go berserk so its pretty much useless.

[]:             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Tue Oct 21, 2014 1:11 am EvangelionGodMode wrote: Nope, what would it have done against bardiel or armisael who infect the evas. Also gendo and seele sabotaged the jet alone project so the united nations or whoever it was wouldn't get rid of their machines that they wanted to use to rule the world and become god. The jet alone coulda been a good dummy against arael, but to put it up against angels like ramiel and zeruel, plus no A/T field, theres no chance. Also i seem to remember that a couple of the angels where only able to be beaten by Unit 01 going berserk, jet alone can't go berserk so its pretty much useless.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Oct 21, 2014 1:28 am There's an advantage to having mechanical fighters over human ones. While Tokita was a jerk in the way that he put it, there's something to be said in a machine that will do exactly what you tell it. It may lack human ingenuity, but sometimes, blind obedience is more than enough to get the job done.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Oct 21, 2014 1:33 am It wouldn't be worthless, no, but it was viewed as a threat because of budget issues. Namely that if various governments saw it (incorrectly) as a viable alternative it could have made it much more difficult to keep the necessary funding levels available.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Oct 21, 2014 1:36 am Interesting thought here. How much cheaper might it be to build something like JA than an Evangelion? If a government spec'd into building multiple JAs, it might be enough to give an Angel a run for its money. By economic standards, it would be the path to take: competition yielding the most monetarily efficient method to deal with the Angels. Of course, that line of action wouldn't be desirable to Gendo or Seele-- but it could have worked.
